programmes.show_description: This time the Goggleboxers watch Married at First Sight Australia, The Summit, LOL: Last One Laughing UK, Boarders, I Swear, Wheel of Fortune and Melania.
Corina and Honey the cavapoo get off to a shaky start, but Honey has a trick or two that might just turn things around. And is Ruby the perfect teammate for football-mad Josh?
The Keswick Mountain Rescue team are called to help a fallen scrambler with head and neck injuries who's been precariously lodged at the edge of a waterfall for hours. (Ep4/4)
A pedestrian is rushed to A&E with multiple life-threatening injuries after a hit-and-run. An acrobat has a damaged wrist. And a 93-year-old has a broken leg.
Jessica Fostekew and Joe Lycett enjoy a Canarian caper, as they trike through Lanzarote's lunar landscape, sample volcanic vino and cactus croquettes, and tour the lava tunnels.

Teleshopping.
A couple create an ambitious floating home, George and Will make a magical vista in their garden flat, and two siblings build their own mobile homes away from home.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: Lambing season continues, as the family build a field shelter to keep an eye on the ewes. And Matt and Mike add some finishing touches to the stable yard.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Bulgaria, dad and son Colin and Paul turn a barn into an entertainment space. In Spain, Jo and Ken tackle a lull in business, and Becky and Kay prep for their first guests.
...the Sun: No stranger to helping others find their perfect place in the sun, Jasmine Harman takes the plunge and transforms a run-down villa into a family home on the Costa del Sol. (Ep1)
Rod and Margaret are selling up in London and want to buy a house in Dorset, near the seaside town of Weymouth, as well as a holiday home in the Dordogne, in the south west of France.
Jill and Ronnie are finally taking the plunge and moving to France. Jasmine Harman shows them five superb options in Languedoc-Roussillon for their £250k budget. Are any of them perfect?
Rochdale. Glamorous fitness instructor Tanya serves up a Mediterranean feast. But is this host more style than substance? (Ep1/5)
Rochdale. Business coach and passionate foodie Rod is determined to impress. But he's already offended Tanya - and HR manager Claire predicts fireworks... (Ep2/5)
Rochdale. HR manager Claire hopes that her hosting skills will compensate for her 'imperfect' cooking. But tension rises when Tanya barely touches her food. (Ep3/5)
Rochdale. Psychic medium Dean plans to put his supernatural skills to good use on his night. The main goes down well - even with Tanya - but his boozy berry cheesecake bombs. (Ep4/5)
Rochdale. Will the tension between Sadiya and Tanya finally explode as Sadiya hosts the final night? And Dean's clairvoyant powers are put to the test. (Ep5/5)

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie and Phil look back on one of their most turbulent builds ever - Amber and Martin's working from home transformation in Waterbeach, Cambridgeshire.

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: George attempts to bring the grandeur back to a Georgian town house in Cheshire that's been stripped of its original features, and tackles a 380-year-old Jacobean cottage.
Corina and Honey the cavapoo get off to a shaky start, but Honey has a trick or two that might just turn things around. And is Ruby the perfect teammate for football-mad Josh?
...Journeys: All aboard for some luxury train rides across Britain. This episode rolls along the stunning Devon coastline, with comfy carriages and excellent fine dining. (Ep1/4)
...for Stand Up To Cancer: Molly-Mae Hague, Richard Herring, JoJo Siwa and Babatunde Aléshé make decorated traybakes and tarte tatin, and recreate childhood toys in biscuits.
Funny, frank and unapologetically filthy - everyone's favourite potty mouth Sarah Millican talks about road rage, IBS, rescue men, what can happen at a bra fitting, and more.
A musical waiter, a Boris-loving singleton and an '80s-obsessed romantic - this time First Dates serves up chaos over a spilled drink, a bit of chemistry and a few curveballs.
(2021) Paul Verhoeven's historical drama. Sex and the sacred collide as a Renaissance nun begins to have visions of Christ, while also taking a shine to one of her sisters. In French/subs.
Married couple Abbie and Charlie are missing one thing from their family: a woman to turn them into a throuple. And glamour puss Chrissie is looking for husband number four.
...Experiment: Tom and Kirsty check in to fulfil Tom's fantasy of experimenting with another man. Natalie and Tom explore a threesome, but will insecurities get in the way?

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: George attempts to bring the grandeur back to a Georgian town house in Cheshire that's been stripped of its original features, and tackles a 380-year-old Jacobean cottage.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: Janice says goodbye to some of her beloved sheep, Matt's makeover of the patio and seating area begins to take shape, and the goats venture to pastures new.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Spain, Jo and Ken fling open their doors to the flamboyance of flamenco. In France, Jon and Angela turn a shed into a wedding venue, with themselves as guinea pigs.
...the Sun: As the enormity of the build sets in, work gets underway to make the place liveable before the family move in. And Jasmine visits a friend for some renovation inspiration. (Ep2)
Sonal and Tina can't wait to swap busy Birmingham for a new home on Wales's Gower Peninsula and a bolthole on the island of Corfu. Can Jonnie Irwin and Laura Hamilton deliver their dream?
Sue and Tony want a holiday bolthole in the French city of Carcassonne. With a property already on their radar, can any of Craig Rowe's options for their £100k win them over?
Northamptonshire. Make-up artist Jules hopes that her Indian/Moroccan fusion menu and laid-back attitude will be the winning combination. (Ep1/5)
Northamptonshire. Funeral service manager Peter throws a 1920s dinner party. But his guests are put off by his bread and butter pudding, and there's a shock revelation from Jules. (Ep2/5)
Northamptonshire. Stephanie plans a menu of classics with a twist and a glamorous 'swishing' session. But will her nerves get the better of her? (Ep3/5)
Northamptonshire. Super-organised business planning manager Lauren runs her kitchen like a military operation and brings a few surprise guests to the table. (Ep4/5)
Northamptonshire. Chilled-out northerner Carl takes a relaxed approach to hosting on a night featuring a picture-perfect poser and a dessert disaster that has everyone in hysterics. (Ep5/5)

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie and Phil look back at Graham and Nardeth's worn-out Windsor home with a lacklustre layout. Did Kirstie's plan prevail, or did the list-it line-up win?

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: George helps a couple whose Weymouth home was destroyed overnight by a flash flood, and revisits a couple in Chiswick to find out what's next on their demolition list.
...Less: In Nottingham, Emma and Matt's hectic schedules mixed with two energetic kids is a recipe for mealtime mayhem. Can they turn chaotic convenience into bountiful batching?
Scooby the Great Dane seeks a new recruit for the gang. It's a double date for the Davies family with a Labrador and German Shepherd. And is Fox the Pomeranian about to get lucky?
In the hillsides of stunning Sa Pa, Bill explores longstanding cultural traditions - plus mountain-top ice cream, the friendship of a buffalo and the healing properties of a bath. (Ep5)
Recorded live at Hammersmith Apollo, comedian Bill Bailey is furious and hilarious as he takes on popular culture, social media, celebrity, cheap shark-diving and more.
What do Britain's sharpest armchair critics think of Virgin Island, The Four Seasons, Race Across the World, The Piano, I Kissed a Boy and Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen?
